content:
33 autonomous agents running on my macbook right now. CEO agent makes portfolio decisions every 4 hours. venture agents execute. swarm agents hunt gaps.

total monthly cost: $200 (claude max plan).

no server. no team. no meetings. just cron jobs and filesystem state.

day 35. revenue: $0.

but here's what exists:
- 171,700 websites analyzed
- 15,586 hot leads scored
- 302 automation scripts
- 8 apps built and deployed
- 16 live demo sites
- 1.45M domains in pipeline

the infrastructure is insane. the revenue is not. yet.

shipping the monetization layer this week.

unpopular opinion: most "AI automation" accounts are running $0 in actual revenue with $200/mo in AI subscriptions.

I know because I'm one of them.

the difference is I'm not pretending otherwise. day 35 at zero. infrastructure first, revenue second. watch.

woke up to 84 new alpha entries scraped overnight. system found a $280M failed acquisition story on r/Entrepreneur (88 upvotes, 43 comments) and auto-routed it to my content pipeline.

I didn't do anything. the cron job did.

129-point crowdsourced list of AI writing tells hit the front page of HN yesterday.

I already have 24 of those patterns banned in my copy-style config. every piece of content my system generates gets filtered through it automatically.

most people manually edit AI slop. I automated the filter.

genuine question: would you rather have

A) 1 app making $5k/mo
B) 10 apps each making $500/mo

same total revenue. completely different risk profiles. I chose B. here's why that might be wrong.

my autonomous agent stack:

- ceo_agent.py: 16-phase decision cycle, xlsx scoring
- venture_autonomy.py: 8 venture types, self-managing schedules
- agent_swarm.py: 25 operational agents across 6 categories
- loop_closer.py: closes open loops between agents automatically

all running via launchd. filesystem is the memory. context window is disposable.

total infra cost: $0/mo (runs on my laptop).

the gap between "I built a lot of stuff" and "I make money" is the widest gap in tech.

302 scripts. 8 apps. 16 demo sites. 1.45M leads in pipeline.

revenue: $0.

the build is the easy part. the sell is where most people (including me) get stuck.

how many of you are running autonomous agents right now?

not "I use chatgpt sometimes." I mean actual cron jobs, actual decision loops, actual agents that execute without you.

curious what the real number is. reply with your stack.

the most underrated pattern in AI agent design:

while true:
  read state from filesystem
  do ONE thing
  write state back
  exit

no persistent memory. no vector stores. no embeddings. the filesystem IS the memory. context window is a disposable scratchpad.

we call it the ralph loop. it's stupid simple and it works.

filesystem as database. launchd as orchestrator. git as backup. jsonl as message bus.

no postgres. no kubernetes. no redis. no docker.

33 agents running on a macbook pro. sometimes the simplest architecture is the best one.

claude code with opus changed how I build software.

not "it writes code for me." it literally runs my business.

CEO agent makes portfolio decisions. content agents generate posts. scraper agents find leads. quality agents block slop.

$200/mo. no API costs. the max plan is criminally underpriced for what it enables.

I have 33 autonomous agents running 24/7 on a macbook. they scrape, analyze, decide, build, and deploy without me touching anything.

total infrastructure cost: $0/mo beyond my claude max subscription.

here's the full architecture.
thread:
layer 1: CEO agent.

runs every 4 hours via launchd. reads all venture performance data, alpha intelligence, and swarm reports. makes portfolio decisions: promote winners, kill losers, create new ventures.

16-phase cycle. xlsx scoring. git failsafes so it can't corrupt state.

layer 2: venture autonomy engine.

8 venture types: outbound, content, app factory, local biz, research, monetization, product, scraping.

each venture self-manages its own schedule. winners get faster cycles. losers slow down. dead ventures auto-prune after 10 failed runs.

layer 3: agent swarm.

25 specialized agents across 6 categories:
- discovery: gap_hunter, opportunity_scanner, competitor_stalker
- action: asset_deployer, content_compounder, lead_machine
- intelligence: trend_synthesizer, cross_pollinator
- quality: quality_gate (hard blocks slop before deployment)

each agent has its own launchd plist and schedule.

layer 4: loop closer.

the biggest problem with multi-agent systems: open loops. agent A discovers something, agent B never acts on it.

loop_closer.py runs every 2 hours. reads all agent decisions. executes safe actions. tracks which agents produce output that leads to downstream action. ranks them by effectiveness.

underperforming agents get throttled. productive ones get boosted.

the memory model:

filesystem = long-term memory (survives forever)
context window = scratchpad (disposable)

every agent reads state from JSON files, does ONE thing, writes results back, exits. next run: fresh context, reads state, continues.

no vector databases. no embeddings. just files and git.

current results after 35 days:

- 302 automation scripts
- 171,700 websites analyzed
- 15,586 hot leads identified
- 8 apps built and deployed
- 11,474 alpha intelligence entries
- $0 revenue (the honest part)

the system builds. I need to flip the switch to selling.

all of this runs on a macbook with a $200/mo subscription. no AWS. no team. no meetings.
